title: Example on basic setup tutorial is wrong
body:
### Issue Description

The example in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman_tutorial.md#inspecting-a-running-container is wrong, but I can't suggest a fix because I don't see what the grep is actually trying to do. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

See https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman_tutorial.md#inspecting-a-running-container

### Describe the results you received

```
-bash: SecondaryIPAddresses:: command not found
-bash: IPAddress:: command not found
```

### Describe the results you expected

Not sure

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
It seems that you're trying to copy and paste the whole snippet and running it as a command because the command itself works fine both on zsh and bash. Can you confirm what exactly you ran?

```
podman inspect basic_httpd | grep IPAddress\":
```

That is exactly right. I clicked the "Copy" button that appears. FWIW, I completely understand what is wrong; I'm proposing that the example be fixed so that on the command to be copied is copied, and that the results are shown separately.
Fair enough, I see that @Honny1 has raised a PR with the required fix.
